hard_signals: Benign conversational text block (meeting scheduling, personal reflection) prepended before promotional/scam content - used as filter evasion
CSS class names using random nature/object word combinations (e.g., 'nebula-quartz', 'pebble-orbit', 'aurora-cinder', 'thistle-comet') in HTML emails
Homoglyph substitution in subject lines: 'I' replaced with 'l' (pIan, TooI, compIimentary), '0' replaced with 'O' (0nly, 35OOWatt, 1OO)
soft_signals: Artificial scarcity claims with specific round numbers: 'Total allocation: 500 sets', 'Total allocation: 800 kits'
Subject line pattern: urgency word + 'Today' or 'Tomorrow' combined with brand impersonation
Body text offering premium gifts as apology for fabricated service failures (lost packages, service issues)
Mismatch between casual/personal opening paragraphs and promotional closing content
Phrases like 'You may qualify', 'eligible recipients', 'residents in your area' combined with free item offers
Medicare kit offers combined with coverage plan change notifications
Points/rewards expiration urgency with specific dollar amounts ($100)
known_sending_infrastructure: Relay hostname patterns: [word][number].[domain].com (e.g., token9709, solid7134, copper6141, flame4339)

Sending domains using compromised/unrelated legitimate-looking domains (ligamorazan.com, galluplionsclubrodeo.com, comandosfilmes.com, tenislibre.com, benjaminrausseo.com, macadomi.com, dalhfuneralchapel.com, jawwhiskey.com, shopgreatescape.com, factorypricedjewelry.com, beachhouseshakes.com, instrumentacionanalitica.com, bubcash.com, crusincountryradio.com, bonusfivicai.com, asianeyestry.com, lemegethat.com)
signals.learner_notes: Derived from sample spam patterns. Will expand as users forward 'Fwd: SPAM Example' emails.
